subject: Physics
title: Edison's Phonograph
description: A micro-lesson that makes Edison's Phonograph usable.

hook.text: In 1877, Thomas Edison recorded and played back sound for the first time. He shouted 'Mary had a little lamb.'

buildup.text: Sound vibrations moved a needle that carved grooves into a rotating cylinder wrapped in tin foil.

discovery.text: Playing it back reversed the process. The needle followed the grooves and reproduced the original vibrations.

twist.text: Edison thought the phonograph's best use would be recording last wills and testaments, not music.

climax.text: The phonograph launched the entire recorded music industry—now worth over $25 billion a year.

punchline.text: A needle, a groove, and a nursery rhyme. Sound recording was born.

quiz.question: How did Edison's phonograph record sound?
options: Sound vibrations moved a needle that carved grooves into tin foil
It converted sound into electrical signals on tape
It captured sound using magnetic fields
